message:
fix: use saturating_sub for all timestamp arithmetic across core crate

Systematic audit of all i64 timestamp subtractions in the core crate.
Bare `-` on i64 timestamps can panic in debug mode or produce incorrect
results with clock skew (NTP corrections, VM migration). All instances
now use saturating_sub for defense-in-depth.

Files fixed:
- experience.rs: resolution_latency_micros() (r - created_ts)
- atc_contamination.rs: rapid-event interval check (ts - last_event)
- atc_risk_budgets.rs: cooldown elapsed check (now - cooldown_started)
- atc_voi_control.rs: ExperimentBudget::is_expired (now - window_start)

Note: atc_effect_semantics.rs line 461 (remaining_micros = cooldown - elapsed)
was audited but is safe — the subtraction is guarded by `elapsed < cooldown`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
